scheme_name: Marriage Assistance To Daughters Of Differently Abled Parents (Parinayam-I)
page_title: Marriage Assistance To Daughters Of Differently Abled Parents (Parinayam-I)

details: The scheme “Marriage Assistance to Daughters of Differently Abled Parents (Parinayam-I)” was launched by the Department of Social Justice, Government of Kerala. Under this scheme, the Kerala Government provides marriage assistance to the daughters of differently-abled parents. The financial aid of ₹30,000/- will be provided for the marriage of up to two daughters of differently abled parents.
benefits: 1. A one-time assistance of ₹30,000/- is provided to differently-abled parents for the marriage of their daughters.
eligibility: 1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Kerala.
2. The differently abled parents for the marriage of their daughters should be eligible to apply under the scheme.
3. While submitting the application for marriage assistance the bride should have completed 18 years of age.
4. The combined annual family income should not exceed ₹1,00,000/-.
Applications for assistance can be submitted for the marriage of two daughters.
The financial assistance for a second child's marriage can be submitted only after 3 years after the initial grant of allowance.

After receiving the financial aid as per these terms, if the marriage has to be separated for any reason and the girl has to marry a second time, then the financial aid can be given for the second marriage. In such cases, the annual income of the family should be calculated taking into account the compensation received from the ex-husband.

If the applicant with disabilities dies before the marriage of the daughters, the said financial assistance can be given to the elder member of the family or any other member of the family who is responsible for conducting the marriage.

In case the applicant PWD dies after the marriage of his daughter but before receiving financial assistance, the senior member of the family or the married member or the individual can be given financial assistance on adequate security.

offline: Registration Process:
Step 01:
To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the applicant needs to visit SUNEETHI- Online Service Application Portal
Step 02:
On the home page, click on “One time Registration”
Step 03:
Fill in the details like Name, Email ID & Mobile Number and click on ‘Register’.
Step 04:
The applicant needs to enter the verification code received on the mobile number and click on ‘Verify’.
Step 05:
Now, a form will open to create the new profile, fill in all the mandatory details and click on ‘Register’.
Login/Application Process:
Once the registration process is completed, the applicant should visit
SUNEETHI- Online Service Application Portal
and click on “Citizen Login”.
Enter the user name i.e. Mobile Number and Password and click on “Sign in”.
Now, the applicant needs to update all their details i.e. Personal Information, Contact Details, Identity Cards & Bank Details by clicking on the 'Edit' button.
Once the applicant updated their profile successfully, the schemes will appear on the left side of the screen.
Select the scheme and click on the “Apply Now” button.
Step 06:
Fill in all the mandatory details in the application form and click on the ‘Submit’ button.
Note:

The application should be submitted at least one month before the date fixed for the marriage. But if the bride is from a Muslim community, the date of the marriage after the religious ceremony called Nikah should be considered for determining the period of application.

documents_required: Birth Certificate (from Corporation/ Municipality/Panchayath) showing that the bride is the daughter of the disabled person
Family Income certificate from the concerned Village Officer
Bank details- Copy of Pass Book

faqs.question: What is the “Marriage Assistance To Daughters Of Differently Abled Parents (Parinayam-I)” Scheme?
faqs.answer: Under this scheme, the Kerala government provides marriage assistance to the daughters of differently-abled parents. The financial aid of ₹30,000/- will be provided for the marriage of up to two daughters of differently abled parents.

faqs.question: Which department has launched this scheme?
faqs.answer: Social Justice Department, Government of Kerala

faqs.question: Who is eligible to get the benefits under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The differently abled parents for the marriage of their daughters should be eligible to apply under the scheme.

faqs.question: Is this scheme only for daughters of differently-abled parents?
faqs.answer: Yes, this scheme is only for differently-abled parents for the marriage of their daughters.

faqs.question: For the marriage of how many daughters the applicant will get financial assistance?
faqs.answer: The applicant will get financial assistance for the marriage of two daughters.

faqs.question: When will the applicant get the financial assistance for the marriage of his/her second daughter?
faqs.answer: The financial assistance for a second child's marriage can be submitted only after 3 years after the initial grant of allowance.

faqs.question: Is this scheme only for a resident of Kerala State?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant should be a permanent resident of Kerala State.

faqs.question: What is the benefit of the scheme?
faqs.answer: Under the scheme, a one-time assistance of ₹30,000/- is provided to differently-abled parents for the marriage of their daughters.

faqs.question: Is there any criterion for annual family income?
faqs.answer: Yes, the combined annual family income should not exceed ₹1,00,000/-.

faqs.question: How can an applicant apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: To avail of the benefits of the scheme, the applicant should visit the Official Portal- SUNEETHI- Online Service Application Portal. https://suneethi.sjd.kerala.gov.in

faqs.question: In how many days should the applicant submit the application form?
faqs.answer: The application should be submitted at least one month before the date fixed for the marriage. But if the bride is from a Muslim community, the date of the marriage after the religious ceremony called Nikah should be considered for determining the period of application.
